name: RethinkDB
description: Open-source distributed document database built from the ground up for realtime push queries

license.comment: Licensed by Linux Foundation under Apache 2.0 after CNCF acquired the source code in 2017

hosting-options.comment: Self-hosted is primary option; third-party managed available via Stackhero, Compose, and RethinkDB Cloud (Heroku add-on). No official managed service.

initial-release.comment: Y Combinator seed funded in 2009; first public release in 2012

data-model.comment: Stores JSON documents with dynamic schemas

topology.comment: Data is assigned to a primary server for each shard; uses Raft for cluster consensus and automatic failover

sharding.comment: Currently implements range shards with automatic split point determination. Up to 64 shards per table.

replication-factor.comment: Replication factor set via web UI or ReQL reconfigure command

multi-region.comment: Uses same protocol for intra-datacenter and cross-datacenter communication. Immediate consistency avoids quorum latency issues common in Dynamo-style systems.

cap-position.comment: Prioritizes consistency over availability; may reject writes during partitions. Trades write availability for data consistency.

consistency-model.comment: Linearizability guaranteed for individual atomic operations on individual documents with proper settings. Read modes: majority (strongest), single, outdated.

acid-transactions.comment: Operations are never atomic across multiple keys. RethinkDB explicitly states it cannot be considered an ACID database.

transaction-scope.comment: Linearizability only for atomic operations on individual documents; no multi-document transactions

isolation-levels.comment: No traditional isolation levels; single-document atomicity only

distributed-transactions.comment: No cross-node transaction coordination; single-document atomicity only

query-language.comment: ReQL (RethinkDB Query Language) - chainable, embeddable query language built into drivers. Not SQL-based.

sql-compatibility.comment: Uses ReQL, a custom query language; no SQL interface

secondary-indexes.comment: Supports simple, compound, multi, and arbitrary expression-based secondary indexes

joins.comment: Distributed joins via eqJoin (efficient, uses indexes), inner_join, and outer_join. Commands automatically distributed across nodes.

aggregations.comment: Supports map-reduce, group-map-reduce, and aggregation functions

full-text-search.comment: No native full-text search. Elasticsearch integration via River plugin was recommended but is incompatible with RethinkDB 2.2+.

max-cluster-size.comment: BigchainDB benchmarked on 32-server cluster on EC2; official benchmarks up to 16 nodes

write-throughput.comment: BigchainDB measured >1M writes/sec on 32-node EC2. Official benchmark: 100K+ ops/sec mixed 50:50 workload on 16-node cluster.

read-latency-p99.comment: RethinkDB 2.1.5 benchmark: p95 latency 3ms for heavy read workloads forbidding stale reads on 16-node cluster

auto-scaling.comment: Manual sharding/replication changes via web UI or ReQL commands. Can be automated via scripts and config management tools.

hot-spot-handling.comment: Split points not automatically changed after table creation. Manual rebalancing via rebalance command when needed.

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Rolling upgrades possible with replicated cluster

online-schema-changes.comment: Schema-less document model allows adding fields without downtime. Index creation is online.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Backup via dump/restore commands creates point-in-time snapshots but no continuous PITR capability

backup-strategy.comment: Hot backups via rethinkdb dump command; creates tar.gz archive of JSON documents. Uses MVCC so doesn't lock clients.

kubernetes-operator.comment: No official K8s operator. Community Helm charts available (Pozetron fork of deprecated official chart) using StatefulSets.

dotnet-driver.comment: Multiple community C# drivers available (bchavez, mfenniak)

orm-support.comment: No major ORM support due to custom ReQL query language

open-source.comment: Apache 2.0 license - true open source. Owned by Linux Foundation.

free-tier.comment: No official managed service. Third-party providers may offer trials but no permanent free tier.

pricing-model.comment: Third-party managed services typically charge per-node/per-hour. Self-hosted is free.

cost-estimate-small.comment: Varies by provider. Self-hosted has no licensing cost. Stackhero and similar providers charge based on resources.

change-data-capture.comment: Changefeeds are RethinkDB's core realtime feature - stream changes from tables, documents, or query results

watch-subscribe.comment: changes() command returns cursor that streams inserts, updates, deletes in realtime. Include_initial option available. Server buffers up to 100K elements.

triggers.comment: No server-side triggers. Triggers were proposed but not implemented. Changefeeds are the recommended alternative for reactive functionality.

event-streaming.comment: Kafka integration via Stream Reactor connectors (Lenses.io) - both source and sink connectors available
